Title: Executive Dependence of Judicial Enforcement
Audit Status: T4 publication-ready audit complete; external validation pending
Score Basis: T4 formula rebaseline: structural 8/8, evidence 11/12, legal fit 7/10, prior proposal 5/8, remedy 11/12, implementation 6/8, abuse resistance 7/8, drafting 8/8, cogency 6/6, adoption 5/12, project integration 4/4, external review 0/4, penalties 0; total 78. T4 added hostile legislative-comparator analysis, source-hierarchy review, and publication-readiness blockers.
Next Audit Need: Run external validation or T4 follow-up after qualified legal or legislative-counsel review; official court-source substitution for D.C. Circuit/Boasberg, Abrego Garcia, Melgar-Salmeron, and New Jersey U.S. Attorney examples where available; fuller supportive-prior-proposal survey; Judgment Fund and indemnification memorandum; and final constitutional review of personal financial liability for federal officers.
Rebaseline Notes: Current under rubric 2026-06-27.2 after T4 component formula rebaseline; internally Review Ready but not publication-ready because external/professional review, official court-source substitution, supportive prior-proposal survey, and personal-payment legal review remain pending.
Adoption Friction Notes: High resistance expected because the proposal imposes personal financial consequences on named executive officials, constrains executive noncompliance tactics, and likely draws separation-of-powers objections and litigation risk; T4 identified hostile recent legislative activity around contempt enforceability.
Enactment Pathway Notes: Ordinary federal legislation likely requires a Senate environment capable of overcoming filibuster constraints. T4 confirms staged vehicles should preserve responsible-official designation, sworn compliance reporting, clear order-specific duties, Judgment Fund limits, and appropriations oversight if the full personal-payment model proves too difficult.

Title: Appointer-President Recusal
Audit Status: T4 publication-ready audit complete; external validation pending
Score Basis: T4 formula rebaseline: structural 8/8, evidence 10/12, legal fit 8/10, prior proposal 5/8, remedy 10/12, implementation 6/8, abuse resistance 6/8, drafting 8/8, cogency 6/6, adoption 5/12, project integration 4/4, external review 0/4, penalties 0; total 77. T4 added close prior-proposal family analysis through Supreme Court ethics and recusal legislation, source-hierarchy review, and publication-readiness blockers.
Next Audit Need: Run external validation or T4 follow-up after qualified legal or legislative-counsel review; primary source substitution for Cannon-related examples; official bill and committee-record support for prior ethics/recusal legislation; local assignment-rule review; small-court and emergency-matter implementation analysis; and final constitutional review of congressional regulation of recusal and reassignment.
Rebaseline Notes: Current under rubric 2026-06-27.2 after T4 component formula rebaseline; internally Review Ready but not publication-ready because external/professional review, primary source substitution, official prior-bill records, local assignment-rule review, and constitutional implementation analysis remain pending.
Adoption Friction Notes: Extreme resistance because the proposal regulates judicial assignment and recusal in presidential-party litigation, may draw separation-of-powers and judge-shopping objections, can be perceived as case-specific despite neutral framing, and analogous Supreme Court ethics legislation advanced on a party-line committee vote.
Enactment Pathway Notes: Ordinary federal legislation likely requires a Senate environment capable of overcoming filibuster constraints. T4 confirms staged alternatives should include Tier 1-only mandatory recusal, Tier 1 plus narrow Tier 2, Judicial Conference study or guidance, local-rule models, and disclosure or reassignment procedures.
